title: Out-of-bounds read in HTTP method handling causes undefined behavior and potential crash This is sharp, Gaurav. We‚Äôve got a real memory-safety bug ins

profile.name: curl
profile.about: cURL is an Open Source project providing a library and command-line tool doing internet transfers

vulnerability_information:
Summary
-‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå Component: libcurl core HTTP handling (HTTP/2 request translation and CONNECT detection)
- Type: out-of-bounds read resulting from missing null-termination
- Impact: Behavior not defined by the specification, the program can crash (DoS) and CONNECT requests can be misclassified
- Root cause: The method string was copied without a null ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äåtermin

Affected‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå code paths
- Struct layout:
- struct httpreq { ... char method[1]; }
- Allocation and copy (no NUL):
- req = calloc(1, sizeof(*req) + m_len);
- memcpy(req->method, method, m_len);

- Unsafe uses:
- strcmp("CONNECT", req->method) ‚Äî assumes null-terminated string
- strlen(req->method) ‚Äî sizes HTTP/2 pseudo-header :method

Direct risk: Both strcmp and strlen may access memory beyond what has been allocated if req->method[m_len] is out-of-bounds. The impact varies with the allocator/layout; ASan will always detect ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äåthis.

Steps‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå to reproduce (concise)

- Prereqs: make sure CMake and nghttp2 are installed
- What version outputs are you having:
```bash
 cmake --version 
```
```text
‚Üí 3.26.2
```

```bash
pkg-config --modversion libnghttp2 
```
```text
‚Üí 1.52.0
```

Build curl with ASan + HTTP/2:


```bash
mkdir -p build && cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug -DCMAKE_C_FLAGS='-O1 -g -fsanitize=address' -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS='-fsanitize=address' -DCURL_USE_NGHTTP2=ON
```
```bash
cmake --build build -j"$(nproc)"
```
Verify binary and features:

```bash
./src/curl ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå--version
```
{F5027206}

## Impact

Impact

-‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå Denial of Service/crash: Out of bounds read occurring during the creation of HTTP/2 pseudo-headers or checking CONNECT logic.
-‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå Logic flaw: The function for detecting CONNECT might behave differently due to a comparison of an uninitialized value being performed.

- Trigger surface: Any path which produces a struct httpreq (e.g., custom methods) and then executes HTTP/2 translation or CONNECT ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äåchecks

Proposed‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå repair (minimal and robust)

- In both creators, not only add a null terminator but also allocate memory for ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äåit:

```diff
- req = calloc(1, sizeof(*req) + m_len);
+ req = calloc(1, sizeof(*req) + m_len + 1);
  if(!req) goto out;
  memcpy(req->method, method, m_len);
+ req->method[m_len] = '\0';
```

-‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå Locations to change:
Curl_http_req_make
Curl_http_req_make2
This change removes local undefined reads without changing the logic. Optional hardening: replace ‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äå‚Äã‚Äç‚Äã‚Äå‚Äç‚Äã‚Äç‚Äåstrcmp("CONNECT",
